segments.text: High-witness news reporter Dan Coob is live in the Sad Center in Dan.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: I'm talking about Mayor Bloomberg. A billionaire who calls women fat broads and horse-faced lesbians.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_03
segments.text: And no, I'm not talking about Donald Trump, I'm talking about Mayor Bloomberg.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: Peggy Rekula was eating lunch at the court diner in media trying to digest her first

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: exposure to Michael Bloomberg.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: I don't hold out much hope for him.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: One man we spoke with described the Democratic debate as a demolition derby.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: Former Philadelphia mayor Michael Nutter is working on the

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: Bloomberg campaign. I would say not our best moment, but getting stronger. Like I said, first debate.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: I'm sure for all the other candidates who have now been debating for eight months, you know, we probably don't

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: remember what that first, what those first debates are like for all of them.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: And so we've got some ground to make up.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: In the latest Quinnipiac poll released Thursday, every Democrat gained ground in a potential general election against President Trump.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: But the Democrats, we spoke to, worry about electability among the six most likely candidates.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: Who's a Democrat? You know, you got, Bloomberg was a Republican,

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04
segments.text: and you got the socialists,

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04
segments.text: and so, we'll say.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_04
segments.text: Mike Bloomberg, the storm,

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: and folks tried to land a couple different punches here and there.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_02
segments.text: Now, Bloomberg's first time on a ballot is not until Super Tuesday,

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: but he will be on the debate stage next week in South Carolina as well. Live in the Sat Center, Dan Coob, CBS 3, Eyewitness News.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_01
segments.text: All right Dan, thank you.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: CBS News will co-host the next Democratic presidential debate in

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Charleston, South Carolina, Gilking and Nora O'Donnell, will moderate. That is

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Tuesday February 25th at 8 p.m. right here on CBS 3.
